name: Botanic Garden of the Jagiellonian University
description: Poland's oldest botanic garden, founded in 1783. 9.6 hectares, ~9,000 plant taxa across alpine gardens, medicinal plants, tropical glasshouses, and an arboretum. A quiet, slow-paced break from the Old Town crowds; the glasshouses are the highlight.
notes: Address: Kopernika 27 · ~25 min walk east of Rynek or one tram stop. Phone +48 12 663 36 35.

items.name: Medicinal plants — herb beds with educational signage
Tropical glasshouses — palms, orchids, water lilies (Victoria amazonica in season)
Arboretum — old-growth trees including 200+ year specimens
Botanical museum — herbarium, illustrations, garden history
